data.title: Barnwell Industries reject Ned Sherwood's attempt to take full control of company
data.description:
• Barnwell Industries (NYSE:BRN) has rejected the attempt by Ned Sherwood and certain of his affiliates to remove and replace the entire Barnwell board of directors.
  • Shareholders not only rejected Sherwood's attempt to seize control of the company, but also supported the Sherwood Group's campaign to remove Douglas Woodrum.
  • The Company is awaiting the Delaware Court of Chancery's decision on the pending lawsuit related to the validity of the Sherwood Group's nomination notice, which purported to nominate directors to stand for election at Barnwell's upcoming 2025 annual meeting of shareholders.
  • Barnwell remains open to engaging in settlement discussions with the Sherwood Group.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stantec Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.16, revenue of C$1.55B
data.description:
• Stantec press release (NYSE:STN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.16.
  • Revenue of C$1.55B (+13.1% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA¹ increase of 19.1% to C$252.3 million and adjusted EBITDA margin¹ of 16.2%, a 70 basis point increase over Q1 2024.
  • Confirms full-year guidance and achieved record backlog of C$7.9 billion, up 12.8% over Q1 2024
  • Acquired Ryan Hanley, a 150-person engineering and environmental consultancy firm in Ireland, strengthening Stantec’s offering in the Irish water sector
  • Entered into a definitive purchase agreement to acquire Page, a 1,400-person US-based design, architecture and engineering firm. The acquisition will position Stantec to become the second largest architecture firm in the US.

  • 2025 Outlook

    Stantec reaffirms the following outlook for 2025:

      2025 Annual Range
    Targets  
    Net revenue growth 7% to 10%
    Adjusted EBITDA as % of net revenue (note) 16.7% to 17.3%
    Adjusted net income as % of net revenue (note) above 8.8%
    Adjusted EPS growth (note) 16% to 19%
    Adjusted ROIC (note) above 12%

data.title: GE Aerospace and Qatar Airways announce partnership with agreement for 400+ GE9X and GEnx engines
data.description:
• GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) and Qatar Airways announced an expansion of their partnership with the signing of multiple deals for new GE9X and GEnx engines.
  • Qatar Airways has signed an agreement for more than 400 engines, including 60 GE9X and 260 GEnx engines, with additional options and spares.
  • The new agreements builds on the previous order for 188 GE9X engines, bringing the total to 248 engines.
  • The addition of GEnx engines for the Boeing 787 fleet supplements their existing 124 engine order.
  • The two deals also include service agreements to cover the maintenance, repair, and overhaul of the GEnx and GE9X engines.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Apple supplier Foxconn receives approval to build fab in India: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) supplier and assembler Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) received approval from India to construct a $435M fabrication plant in India, a move that will help the iPhone maker continue to diversify its supply chain outside of China, according to TechCrunch.

The plant will be located in Uttar Pradesh and is slated for operation in 2027, according to the report. It will eventually produce display driver chips for mobile phones, laptops, PCs and other devices. The plant will initially serve as a semiconductor assembly and test facility before it advances to chip fabrication.

Once the plant reaches the stage of fabrication, it "will have a capacity of 20,000 wafers per month and can produce 36 million units monthly," said India's IT minister Ashwini Vaishnaw, according to the report.

Last year, the Indian government created an incentive program of $15B to encourage the construction of semiconductor manufacturing plants in the country. 

Apple is steadily migrating iPhone assemblage to India, as the Cupertino-based company now produces 20% of its smartphones there, an increase of 60% over the past year.

data.title: Ares Capital snaps six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Ares Capital (NASDAQ:ARCC) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 0.7% lower at $21.63 on Wednesday.

The company gained over 5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has lost 1% so far this year, compared to the nearly 0.3% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

ARCC is up 8% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.8% lower on Tuesday at $21.78.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, ARCC has a Hold rating with a score of 3.11 out of 5. The company received an A for valuation, while it got an F in the prospect of growth.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 11 analysts gave ARCC a Buy and above. Two analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and while none recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Last month, Ares Capital's Q1 earnings missed the Wall Street consensus as interest income from investments missed the average analyst estimate, but net investment income beat.

However, a recent Seeking Alpha analysis by The Asian Investor noted that Ares Capital missed EPS expectations for Q1, but results were nonetheless very robust.

“Ares Capital's diversified portfolio and first lien strategy, combined with 326% Y/Y growth in net commitments, highlight its growth potential,” it added.

data.title: Jack in the Box's soft comp store sales weigh on the top-line
data.description:
Jack in the Box (NASDAQ:JACK) reported mixed fiscal Q2 results as better-than-expected earnings were overshadowed by a miss on the top-line and weak comparable store sales.

Shares are down more than 6% in postmarket trading.

Same-store sales were down 4.4% in FQ2, which consisted of a 4.5% decline in franchise same-store sales and a 4.0% decline in company-owned stores, all of which were worse than estimates of -2.39%, -2.72%, and -2.68%, respectively.

The steeper-than-expected drop in traffic drove revenue down nearly 8% to $336.7M, below the consensus estimate of $3.42.3M, while the company’s profit eroded to $1.20 per share from $1.46 in the same quarter last year, although 5 cents better than expected. Adjusted EBITDA was down 12% to $66.5M.

System-wide same store sales in the company’s Del Taco segment were down 3.6% comprised of a 4.2% drop in franchise store sales and a 1.7% decline in company-operated same-store sales. The soft sales performance resulted from a decline in transactions, partially offset by an increase in price.

For FY25, Jack in the Box maintained its earlier guidance issued on April 23 in which operating EPS, a non-GAAP metric, is expected to be between $5.05 and $5.40, straddling the $5.25 consensus estimate. Adjusted EBITDA is targeted for $282M and $292M. For its Jack in the Box restaurants, the company expects same-store sales of negative low-to-mid-single digits from FY24.

CEO Comment: “I am encouraged by our marketing plans in the back half of 2025, which we expect to energize sales despite the difficult industry-wide macro environment in which we continue to operate. As we stated when announcing the recent 'JACK on Track' plan, we are addressing the areas of need to improve the business, and I am confident in our ability to establish consistent top-line trends while becoming a more simple, efficient company and investor story.”

data.title: CV Sciences targets cost efficiency gains and product diversification with in-source manufacturing and non-cannabinoid launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CV Sciences (CVSI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Joseph Dowling reported that CV Sciences generated revenue of $3.6 million, slightly down from the previous quarter, and achieved a gross margin of 46%. Dowling stated the company continues to "maintain our number one position in the natural product retail channel" and has increased market share in this area. He highlighted a strategic shift: "During Q1, we began to more fully adapt our product innovation efforts to include more non-cannabinoid products to more fully diversify our product offering."
  • Dowling emphasized the company's M&A strategy, noting, "We are looking for opportunities to increase revenue profitably with transactions that can leverage our assets...We believe the timing is right for us to have more control over the manufacturing side of the supply chain." The recent acquisition of Elevated Softgels is expected to improve cost efficiency and gross margin in the second half of 2025. Expansion of contract manufacturing capabilities at the Elevated facility is underway.
  • The launch of the Lunar Fox plant-based foods line in the U.S. was described as an "exciting milestone," with Dowling stating, "Lunar Fox products are now available at select retailers and from the company's new website."
  • The innovation pipeline includes both cannabinoid and non-cannabinoid products, with the new fizz effervescent tablet launched under the Aura collection. Dowling said, "We will continue to innovate and launch new products that are responsive to our customers."
  • Regulatory challenges were acknowledged, but Dowling asserted, "We remain bullish on the US hemp and CBD category" and cited market contraction as an opportunity for CV Sciences to capture share.
  • CFO Joerg Grasser reported, "We generated EBITDA of $131,000 and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $311,000. We maintained strong gross margins of 46% and continued to reduce our operating expenses."

Outlook

  • Management expects improved cost efficiency and gross margin in the second half of 2025 from the integration of Elevated Softgels and increased in-source manufacturing. Dowling said, "We expect to see improved cost efficiency and gross margin in the second half of 2025, directly from the integration of Elevated Softgels."
  • The company anticipates launching several new non-cannabinoid products during the remainder of 2025 under the Plus Health brand.
  • Grasser indicated, "We anticipate some modest cash usage in the very near future until we recognize the synergies of our acquisitions and generate positive cash flow in the second half of 2025."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $3.6 million, compared to $3.9 million in Q4 2024 and $4 million in Q1 2024. Gross margin improved to 46% from 43.2% in Q4 2024. Grasser attributed the sequential decline in revenue to "lower unit sales by 8% in the first quarter of 2025 compared to the fourth quarter of 2024," primarily due to increased state regulations and temporary out-of-stock issues.
  • New products introduced since the start of 2023 represented 35% of revenue for Q1 2025. Direct-to-consumer sales made up 44.8% of total revenue, with B2C revenue declining by 9% sequentially due to seasonality.
  • SG&A expense was $2.1 million, down from both the prior year and the previous quarter, reflecting ongoing cost control. The net loss for the quarter was $0.1 million, compared to a net loss of $0.7 million in Q4 2024. Cash at quarter-end was $0.8 million, up from $0.5 million at year-end, supported by a $1.2 million financing agreement.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: No analyst questions or management responses were included in the current transcript.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' sentiment cannot be assessed due to the absence of Q&A.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in prepared remarks, particularly regarding the potential for improved cost efficiency, scale, and product innovation. Dowling used phrases such as "We are optimistic about the long term opportunity for our company" and "We continue to make tough decisions to ensure that we are focused on adding long term shareholder value."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remains consistently focused on operational improvements and strategic diversification, with perhaps increased emphasis on immediate steps toward profitability and product expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for improved cost efficiency and gross margin in the second half of 2025 was more explicitly stated this quarter. There is an increased focus on in-source manufacturing and non-cannabinoid product innovation compared to Q4 2024, where the outlook was centered on the benefits of recent acquisitions and general cost savings.
  • Revenue declined quarter-over-quarter, while gross margin improved. SG&A expense continued to decrease. The new product contribution to revenue increased from 28% (FY 2024) to 35% (Q1 2025).
  • Management’s confidence in leveraging M&A and product diversification initiatives was reiterated, with a more pronounced emphasis on immediate operational execution.
  • Analysts' tone cannot be compared due to the lack of Q&A in both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory risk was highlighted, with Dowling stating, "Numerous states have passed legislation that are unfavorable to the CBD category." The company is actively involved in lobbying and advocacy to address these challenges.
  • Temporary out-of-stock issues impacted unit sales due to "manufacturing delays from two of our CMOs." Management has addressed these challenges and sees further justification for in-sourcing manufacturing.
  • The company anticipates modest cash usage in the near term as it integrates acquisitions and recognizes synergies.

Final Takeaway

CV Sciences' Q1 2025 call underscored a dual focus on cost efficiency through in-source manufacturing and strategic product diversification, particularly with new non-cannabinoid launches under the Plus Health brand and the Lunar Fox plant-based foods line. Management highlighted improved gross margins and a narrowing net loss, while reiterating confidence in capturing additional market share amid industry contraction and regulatory headwinds. The company expects tangible benefits from acquisition integration and new product momentum to begin materializing in the second half of 2025, positioning CV Sciences for enhanced profitability and operational leverage moving forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: DLocal stock jumps after Q1 earnings beat on strong payment volume
data.description:
DLocal (NASDAQ:DLO) shares jumped 15% in Wednesday after-hours trading after the Uruguay-based payment processing platform recorded stronger-than-expected Q1 2025 earnings on robust payment volume.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.15, topping the $0.11 average analyst estimate, ticked up from $0.10 in the prior quarter and $0.06 a year before.

Revenue of $216.8M in Q1, also exceeding the $209.9M consensus, rose from $204.5M in Q4 2024 and $184.4M in Q1 2024.

Total payment volume was $8.11B in Q1, up from $7.71B in Q4 and $5.31B in the year-ago period. That's well above the $7.71B Visible Alpha estimate

Gross profit advanced to $84.9M from $83.7M in the earlier quarter and $63.0M in 2024's Q1.

Adjusted EBITDA of $57.9M increased from $56.9M in Q4 and $36.8M in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 27% vs. 28% in Q4 and 20% in Q1 2024.

Conference call at 5:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: Discover Financial credit card delinquency rate falls, charge-off rate rises in April
data.description:
Discover Financial Services (NYSE:DFS) credit card delinquency rate improved in April 2025, while its net charge-off rate increased, according to data the company disclosed in a filing on Wednesday. Meanwhile, its lending activity increased by less than 1%.

The delinquency rate of 3.50% improved from 3.66% in March, and stayed below its 3.72% rate in April 2024. However, it remains above the 2.38% delinquency rate in April 2019, before the pandemic.

Its net charge-off rate of 5.04% ticked up from 4.94% in the previous month. The measure remained below its 5.60% rate in last year's April. It compared with 3.46% in April 2019.

Loans at April 30, 2025, stood at $99.5B, up 0.5% M/M and down 0.2% Y/Y.

Discover (NYSE:DFS) stock was little changed in Wednesday after-hours trading.

The company's merger into Capital One Financial (COF) is expected to close on May 18, 2025.
